During his tenure with the UFC, he was awarded "Fight of the Night" honors four times.

**Thiago Tavares** (born November 8, 1984) is a Brazilian [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") and [medical doctor](/wiki/Medical_doctor "Medical doctor") who most recently competed in the [Lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight_(MMA) "Lightweight (MMA)") division. An 18-fight veteran of the [UFC](/wiki/UFC "UFC"), he has also fought in the [Professional Fighters League](/wiki/Professional_Fighters_League "Professional Fighters League") and [Cage Warriors](/wiki/Cage_Warriors "Cage Warriors"). During his tenure with the UFC, he was awarded "Fight of the Night" honors four times.

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiago_Tavares&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Born and raised in [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"), Tavares began training in [judo](/wiki/Judo "Judo") at the age of five and [Brazilian jiu-jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_jiu-jitsu "Brazilian jiu-jitsu") at the age of nine.[[3]](#cite_note-be20-3)

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiago_Tavares&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiago_Tavares&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early career")]

Tavares made his professional MMA debut in November 2003. He amassed an undefeated record of 10–0 in his first three years in the sport before being signed by the UFC.

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiago_Tavares&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

#### Lightweight

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiago_Tavares&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Lightweight")]

Tavares entered the UFC with an undefeated record and was highly regarded. Tavares outgrappled fellow UFC-newcomer [Naoyuki Kotani](/wiki/Naoyuki_Kotani "Naoyuki Kotani") to a unanimous decision, followed by a dominant submission victory over [Jason Black](/wiki/Jason_Black "Jason Black"). Tavares fought perennial contender [Tyson Griffin](/wiki/Tyson_Griffin "Tyson Griffin") to a unanimous decision loss, the first of his professional career, at [UFC 76](/wiki/UFC_76 "UFC 76"). They would win [Fight of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") award honors for their performance.[[4]](#cite_note-mmaj07-4)

Thiago rebounded with a unanimous decision victory over [Michihiro Omigawa](/wiki/Michihiro_Omigawa "Michihiro Omigawa") at [UFC Fight Night: Swick vs Burkman](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night:_Swick_vs_Burkman "UFC Fight Night: Swick vs Burkman"). Tavares was knocked out in his next bout by [Matt Wiman](/wiki/Matt_Wiman "Matt Wiman") and lost a unanimous decision to [Kurt Pellegrino](/wiki/Kurt_Pellegrino "Kurt Pellegrino") nearly three months later. Despite losses, Tavares received [Fight of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") award in both fights.[[5]](#cite_note-mmaj08-5) This marked the first time Tavares lost two straight contests after beginning his career with twelve straight wins.

Tavares defeated [TUF](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter "The Ultimate Fighter") finalist [Manvel Gamburyan](/wiki/Manvel_Gamburyan "Manvel Gamburyan") via unanimous decision at [UFC 94](/wiki/UFC_94 "UFC 94") in a close fight.

Tavares and [Nik Lentz](/wiki/Nik_Lentz "Nik Lentz") fought to a majority draw at [UFC Fight Night: Maynard vs. Diaz](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night:_Maynard_vs._Diaz "UFC Fight Night: Maynard vs. Diaz"). Tavares was docked a point in the third round for a second kick to the groin of Lentz. After the fight Lentz was interviewed and had shared with a reporter his belief that Tavares had intentionally kicked him in the groin and happened to crack Lentz's metal cup.

Tavares was scheduled to face [Melvin Guillard](/wiki/Melvin_Guillard "Melvin Guillard") on May 29, 2010, at [UFC 114](/wiki/UFC_114 "UFC 114"),[[6]](#cite_note-6) but Tavares was forced off the card with an elbow injury.[[7]](#cite_note-7) He was replaced by [Waylon Lowe](/wiki/Waylon_Lowe "Waylon Lowe").

Tavares was set to face [Willamy Freire](/wiki/Willamy_Freire "Willamy Freire") at [UFC Live: Jones vs. Matyushenko](/wiki/UFC_Live:_Jones_vs._Matyushenko "UFC Live: Jones vs. Matyushenko") taking place August 1, 2010.[[8]](#cite_note-8) However, on July 20, 2010, Freire withdrew because of an injury,[[9]](#cite_note-9) forcing the bout to be cancelled.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Tavares faced UFC newcomer Pat Audinwood on September 25, 2010, at [UFC 119](/wiki/UFC_119 "UFC 119"), replacing an injured [Aaron Riley](/wiki/Aaron_Riley "Aaron Riley").[[11]](#cite_note-11) Tavares defeated Audinwood via submission (guillotine choke) in the first round.

Tavares then faced [Shane Roller](/wiki/Shane_Roller "Shane Roller") on March 3, 2011, at [UFC Live: Sanchez vs. Kampmann](/wiki/UFC_Live:_Sanchez_vs._Kampmann "UFC Live: Sanchez vs. Kampmann").[[12]](#cite_note-12) He lost the fight via KO in the second round. Though Tavares hurt Roller in the first round, he was unable to finish him. During the second round, Tavares was caught by a straight right from Roller that knocked him out.

Tavares defeated [Spencer Fisher](/wiki/Spencer_Fisher "Spencer Fisher") via second-round TKO on August 27, 2011, at [UFC 134](/wiki/UFC_134 "UFC 134").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Tavares faced [Sam Stout](/wiki/Sam_Stout "Sam Stout") on January 14, 2012, at [UFC 142](/wiki/UFC_142 "UFC 142").[[14]](#cite_note-14) He won the fight via unanimous decision after utilizing takedowns with his top control for most of the fight.

Tavares was expected to face [Tony Ferguson](/wiki/Tony_Ferguson_(fighter) "Tony Ferguson (fighter)") on May 5, 2012, at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3"), replacing an injured [Dennis Hallman](/wiki/Dennis_Hallman "Dennis Hallman").[[15]](#cite_note-HallmanOut/TavaresIn-15) However, Tavares was forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by [Michael Johnson](/wiki/Michael_Johnson_(fighter) "Michael Johnson (fighter)").[[16]](#cite_note-TavaresOut/JohnsonIn-16)

Tavares was expected to face [Dennis Hallman](/wiki/Dennis_Hallman "Dennis Hallman") on September 1, 2012, at [UFC 151](/wiki/UFC_151 "UFC 151").[[17]](#cite_note-17) However, after [UFC 151](/wiki/UFC_151 "UFC 151") was cancelled, Tavares/Hallman was then rescheduled to October 5, 2012, at [UFC on FX 5](/wiki/UFC_on_FX_5 "UFC on FX 5").[[18]](#cite_note-18) Just prior to the UFC on FX 5 weigh ins, Dennis Hallman alerted officials and Tavares that he would miss weight by 7 pounds. Tavares was given the option to take the fight anyway at a [catchweight](/wiki/Catchweight "Catchweight"). He stated he would only fight if Hallman could make it within 3 lbs of the 156 lb. limit. Hallman couldn't make it in the allowed time and so the bout was cancelled.[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20)

Tavares next faced the undefeated [Khabib Nurmagomedov](/wiki/Khabib_Nurmagomedov "Khabib Nurmagomedov") on January 19, 2013, at [UFC on FX: Belfort vs. Bisping](/wiki/UFC_on_FX:_Belfort_vs._Bisping "UFC on FX: Belfort vs. Bisping").[[21]](#cite_note-21) He lost the fight via knockout early in the first round. Following his loss to Nurmagomedov, it was announced that Tavares had tested positive for the banned substance [drostanolone](/wiki/Drostanolone "Drostanolone") during his post-fight drug test and, subsequently, was suspended for nine months retroactive to January 19.[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23)

Tavares was expected to face [Quinn Mulhern](/wiki/Quinn_Mulhern "Quinn Mulhern") on November 9, 2013, at [UFC Fight Night 32](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_32 "UFC Fight Night 32").[[24]](#cite_note-24) However, Mulhern pulled out of the bout citing an injury and was replaced by [Justin Salas](/wiki/Justin_Salas "Justin Salas").[[25]](#cite_note-MulhernOut-25) Tavares won the fight via submission in the first round.

#### Move to Featherweight

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiago_Tavares&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Move to Featherweight")]

Tavares was expected to face promotional newcomer [Zubaira Tukhugov](/wiki/Zubaira_Tukhugov "Zubaira Tukhugov") in a featherweight bout on February 15, 2014, at [UFC Fight Night 36](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_36 "UFC Fight Night 36").[[26]](#cite_note-26) However, Tavares was forced out of the bout citing a rib injury and replaced by Douglas Silva de Andrade.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

Tavares was expected to face [Tom Niinimäki](/wiki/Tom_Niinim%C3%A4ki "Tom Niinimäki") on May 31, 2014, at [UFC Fight Night 41](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_41 "UFC Fight Night 41").[[28]](#cite_note-28) However, Tavares pulled out of the bout citing another injury[[29]](#cite_note-29) and was replaced by promotional newcomer [Niklas Bäckström](/wiki/Niklas_B%C3%A4ckstr%C3%B6m_(fighter) "Niklas Bäckström (fighter)").[[30]](#cite_note-BäckströmIn-30)

In his eventual featherweight debut, Tavares faced [Robbie Peralta](/wiki/Robbie_Peralta "Robbie Peralta") on August 16, 2014, at [UFC Fight Night 47](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_47 "UFC Fight Night 47").[[31]](#cite_note-31) Tavares won the fight via submission in the first round after completely dominating Peralta on the ground. The win also earned him his first *Performance of the Night* bonus award.[[32]](#cite_note-32)

Tavares was expected to face [Nik Lentz](/wiki/Nik_Lentz "Nik Lentz") in a rematch on February 14, 2015, at [UFC Fight Night 60](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_60 "UFC Fight Night 60").[[33]](#cite_note-33) However, Tavares pulled out of the bout in late January citing yet another injury.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

Tavares was expected to face [Zubaira Tukhugov](/wiki/Zubaira_Tukhugov "Zubaira Tukhugov") on June 6, 2015, at [UFC Fight Night 68](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_68 "UFC Fight Night 68").[[35]](#cite_note-35) However, Tukhugov was replaced by [Brian Ortega](/wiki/Brian_Ortega "Brian Ortega").[[36]](#cite_note-36) Tavares lost the back-and-forth fight via TKO in the third round.[[37]](#cite_note-37) Despite the loss, Tavares was awarded his fourth *Fight of the Night* bonus award.[[38]](#cite_note-38)

Tavares faced [Clay Guida](/wiki/Clay_Guida "Clay Guida") on November 7, 2015, at [UFC Fight Night 77](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_77 "UFC Fight Night 77").[[39]](#cite_note-39) He won the fight with a quick submission in the first round.[[40]](#cite_note-40) The win also earned Tavares his second *Performance of the Night* bonus award.[[41]](#cite_note-41)

Tavares next faced [Doo Ho Choi](/wiki/Doo_Ho_Choi "Doo Ho Choi") on July 8, 2016, at [The Ultimate Fighter 23 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Joanna_vs._Team_Cl%C3%A1udia#The_Ultimate_Fighter_23_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Joanna vs. Team Cláudia")[[42]](#cite_note-42) Tavares lost the fight via KO in the first round.[[43]](#cite_note-43) Afterwards, the promotion did not re-sign Tavares, making him a free agent.[[44]](#cite_note-mmaf17-44)

### Post-UFC career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiago_Tavares&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Post-UFC career")]

After being released from the UFC, Tavares signed with Aspera FC and faced Mauricio Machado at Aspera FC 49 on February 18, 2017.[[44]](#cite_note-mmaf17-44) He won the fight via first-minute knockout.[[45]](#cite_note-tsf17-45)

He was then scheduled to face Fabricio Oliveira at Aspera FC 50 on March 18, 2017, but withdrew from the bout due to an unknown reason.[[45]](#cite_note-tsf17-45)

#### Professional Fighters League

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiago_Tavares&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Professional Fighters League")]

He was then signed to participate the Lightweight division of the [inaugural season](/wiki/Professional_Fighters_League#2018_PFL_inaugural_season "Professional Fighters League") of PFL.[[3]](#cite_note-be20-3) His regular season begun with facing Robert Watley at [PFL 2](/wiki/PFL_2_(2018_season) "PFL 2 (2018 season)") on June 21, 2018. In the second round, Watley unintentionally kicked Tavares in the groin rendering him unable to continue after the five minute recovery time.[[46]](#cite_note-mmaf18-46) Contrary to the unified rules, Watley was declared as the winner.[[46]](#cite_note-mmaf18-46)

His second bout was against Arthur Estrázulas at [PFL 5](/wiki/PFL_5_(2018_season) "PFL 5 (2018 season)") on August 2, 2018. He won the fight via split decision and advanced to the playoffs.[[47]](#cite_note-47)

In the quarter-finals Tavares faced Islam Mamedov at [PFL 9](/wiki/PFL_9_(2018_season) "PFL 9 (2018 season)") on October 13, 2018. He lost the bout via unanimous decision and was eliminated from the playoffs.[[48]](#cite_note-be18-48) However, Mamedov could not continue to the semi-finals and Tavares was brought to replace him against [Rashid Magomedov](/wiki/Rashid_Magomedov "Rashid Magomedov").[[48]](#cite_note-be18-48) Tavares lost the bout via second-round technical knockout and was eliminated from the tournament.[[48]](#cite_note-be18-48) He subsequently retired from the sport in order to focus in his studies.[[3]](#cite_note-be20-3)

### Return from retirement

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiago_Tavares&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Return from retirement")]

Reaching the clinical phase of his studies, Tavares was able to train more consistently again and was scheduled to return from retirement against Laercio Alves dos Santos at Gladiator CF on July 22, 2020. However, the bout was then rescheduled to take place on October 24, 2020. Tavares won the fight via first-round knockout.[[49]](#cite_note-49)

He is next expected to face Jean Carlos Liebl at Gladiator CF 54 on February 6, 2021.[[50]](#cite_note-50)

Then, he is also expected to face Mauricio Ariel for the Super Lightweight Championship at Inside Fighters' League 1 on April 11, 2021.[[51]](#cite_note-51)

### Return to the UFC

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiago_Tavares&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Return to the UFC")]

Tavares was signed to UFC as a ringside doctor in 2025.[[52]](#cite_note-52)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiago_Tavares&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Personal life")]
